close_reason: Implemented by commits 6a5a540 and e6490e0. Review finding remediated; package lint/test/typecheck pass.

comments.text: Implemented and committed sbdpi-qmd.1.1 as 6a5a540 (feat(pi-beadwork): add worker execution mode types for sbdpi-qmd.1.1). Added WorkerExecutionMode/checkout union/runtime base/type guards, worktree narrowing at orchestration callsites, registry round-trip normalization, and focused type guard/serialization tests. Validation run: npm run lint -w @solvedbydev/pi-beadwork-extension passed; npm run typecheck -w @solvedbydev/pi-beadwork-extension passed; targeted vitest for types/worker-diagnostics/worker-manager/index/dashboard passed (56 tests). I did not close the issue because running the broader relevant vitest set including src/__tests__/unit/orchestrator.test.ts fails in pre-existing dirty user changes in that file: the new dirty test "reruns reviewer gating after landing rebase remediation before merge-back" observes timeline ["rebase-failed", "rebase-failed"] vs expected ["rebase-failed"]. I staged only my two fixture lines from orchestrator.test.ts; the rest of that file remains dirty/uncommitted as found.

Review remediation complete: added a focused compile-time `@ts-expect-error` assertion in `types.test.ts` proving `worktreePath` cannot be accessed after narrowing to a current-branch worker. Verified with `npm run test -w @solvedbydev/pi-beadwork-extension -- src/__tests__/unit/types.test.ts`, `npm run typecheck -w @solvedbydev/pi-beadwork-extension`, and `npm run lint -w @solvedbydev/pi-beadwork-extension`. Committed as e6490e0 (`test(pi-beadwork): remediate sbdpi-qmd.1.1 review`).

Coordinator review complete. Implemented by commits 6a5a540 and e6490e0. Review finding for missing compile-time impossible-state coverage was remediated with an @ts-expect-error type-level test. Package gates passed: lint, test (193), typecheck for @solvedbydev/pi-beadwork-extension.

title: Add WorkerExecutionMode discriminated union types to types.ts
